charity.s: $40M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.08% of revenue) via PwC Charitable Foundation.

environment.s: Climate-commitment coalition member: LEAF Coalition (since 2022). LEAF Coalition corporate funder — committed up to $4M.

labor.s: Mental Health at Work Pledge signatory (since 2022): public commitment to gold-standard workplace mental-health policy, C-suite accountability, and measurable outcomes.

dei.s: DiversityInc Top 50 (2024) — workforce diversity + partner pipeline. Seramount Working Mother 100 Best (2024) — paid parental + flexible-schedule programs. [DiversityInc Top 50 · Working Mother 100 Best · Paradigm for Parity signatory]

wiki.industry: professional services industry, accounting services, management consulting industry, audit, consultation
wiki.keyPeople: Samuel Lowell Price, Edwin Waterhouse, William Cooper
wiki.controversies:
===Americanas (AMER3) controversy===
In January 2023, the firm was involved in a controversy when it approved Americanas' (AMER3) balance sheets with accounting inconsistencies of around US$4 billion. This caused volatility to company's price on the Brazilian stock exchange and losses to the company's shareholders. After verifying the impacts caused, the CVM (Brazilian body that regulates the stock exchanges) opened investigations against the company's auditors to determine responsibilities.
wiki.extract: PricewaterhouseCoopers, also known as PwC, is a multinational professional services network based in London, England.
